text: AI is increasingly common in the workplace, changing how many people work. One major impact is automation, where AI handles repetitive tasks like data entry or scheduling. This frees up employees for more creative and strategic work. Productivity tools powered by AI also help workers analyse information faster and make better decisions. For example, AI can sort through customer data to suggest personalised offers. However, this does not mean humans are replaced. Instead, human-AI collaboration is becoming essential. People still bring creativity, empathy, and complex problem-solving skills that AI lacks. These changes mean some jobs will evolve, and workers may need to learn new skills to work alongside AI effectively.
